hero.subtitle_part1: And of that reported data, how much is
questions: Do you truly believe traditional tools like KPI and OKR are enough to manage people effectively?
Can you trace back when and where someone leaked sensitive business information?
Do you find it consistently difficult to collaborate with teams that are not co-located?

current_state.title: Lost Productivity
current_state.stat1: According to Asana: "60% of office workers' time generates no value."
current_state.stat2: According to Microsoft: "57% of employees' daily time is spent on meetings, chat, and email."
current_state.example_result: That's over <1>100M VND/month</1> — <2>1.2B VND/year</2> lost (excluding opportunity costs)
current_state.transition_label: But why do reports always show "100% complete"?

report_vs_reality.title: Reports vs. Reality
report_vs_reality.evidence1: Employees spend <1>1.5–2.5 hours/day</1> on social media & web browsing – Teamstage
report_vs_reality.evidence2: <1>89%</1> of employees waste <2>30 minutes – 2 hours/day</2> – Forbes
report_vs_reality.takeaway: Reports can look <1>polished</1> – but <2>behavioral data</2> tells the real story. No data = leaders only hear what employees <3>want them to hear</3>.

root_cause.title: The Real Root Cause of Every Problem
root_cause.card1_body: No actual progress timeline to trace "who / when / why".
root_cause.card2_body: Tasks, chat, email, files, attendance… scattered everywhere with no single source of truth.
root_cause.card3_title: Leaders are forced to make gut-based decisions
root_cause.card3_body: Unable to measure what's actually happening → wrong priorities → missed deadlines, cost overruns, elevated risk.
root_cause.warning: When you can't see <1>"what's really happening"</1>, every decision becomes a <2>gamble</2>.
root_cause.evidence_stat: According to BARC: <1>58%</1> of business decisions in Vietnam are based on intuition.
root_cause.evidence_close: Without data, businesses are forced to <1>operate on chance</1>.
root_cause.image_alt: Foggy unclear path representing decision making without data

limitations.title: Why Is Managing a Business Still Hard — Even With So Many Tools?
card2_items: Unknown tools, malicious sites, dangerous files
Who is the source of a data leak
limitations.takeaway: This is <1>the most critical data layer</1> for running a business — and it's the layer every organization is missing.
limitations.transition: So what does a business actually need to manage effectively?

why_real_data.subtitle: Without real data, remote management becomes blind and gut-driven
why_real_data.block1_problem_label: In remote/hybrid work, leaders struggle to know:
block1_problems: Whether the team is genuinely working
why_real_data.block1_note: <1>Especially critical for:</1> Outsourced teams, Night shifts, Freelancers, Junior staff
why_real_data.block2_intro: Many problems don't show up in month-end KPIs — they live in <1>daily behavior:</1>
block2_signals: Frequently stepping away from the desk
Handling personal tasks during work hours
why_real_data.block2_ai_note: <1>AI detects abnormal patterns</1> far earlier than end-of-month reports
block3_helps: Alert when staff causes a data leak
Trace back the root cause of an incident
